hook.text: If you've been told you need bowel surgery for endo watch this first
hook.target_audience_raw: endometriosis patients told they need bowel surgery

main_claim.text: Most bowel endometriosis is treated by shaving the surface; it is rare for it to penetrate the full bowel wall, which is when a colorectal surgeon becomes necessary.

supporting_explanation.summary: The video explains a specialized ultrasound (recto-end/anal) that can accurately assess depth of bowel involvement, although it's not widely available. Most cases involve surface or superficial disease, treated by a gynecologic surgeon via shaving. Only when endometriosis goes through the muscularis propria (the 'barrier to poo') does a colorectal surgeon need to be involved, highlighting the importance of pre-surgical planning.

funnel_rationale: Pure educational content clarifying bowel endometriosis management without directing to a specific clinic or service, typical of awareness-building (TOFU).

secondary_raw: recto-end ultrasound, shaving technique, colorectal surgeon involvement
